name: set platform software trace
detail: Set the trace level for a specific module within a process.
syntax: set platform software trace process slot module trace-level

params.name: process
params.description: Process whose tracing level is being set. Options include: • chassis-manager—The Chassis Manager process. • cli-agent—The CLI Agent process. • dbm—The Database Manager process. • emd—The Environmental Monitoring process. • fed—The Forwarding Engine Driver process. • forwarding-manager—The Forwarding Manager process. • host-manager—The Host Manager process. • iomd—The Input/Output Module daemon (IOMd) process. • ios—The IOS process. • license-manager—The License Manager process. • logger—The Logging Manager process. • platform-mgr—The Platform Manager process. • pluggable-services—The Pluggable Services process. • replication-mgr—The Replication Manager process. • shell-manager—The Shell Manager process. • smd—The Session Manager process. • table-manager—The Table Manager Server. • wireless—The wireless controller module process. • wireshark—The Embedded Packet Capture (EPC) Wireshark process. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 2436 System Management set platform software trace

params.name: slot
params.description: Hardware slot where the process for which the trace level is set, is running. Options include: • number—Number of the SIP slot of the hardware module where the trace level is set. For instance, if you want to specify the SIP in SIP slot 2 of the switch, enter 2. • SIP-slot / SPA-bay—Number of the SIP switch slot and the number of the shared port adapter (SPA) bay of that SIP. For instance, if you want to specify the SPA in bay 2 of the SIP in switch slot 3, enter 3/2. • F0—The Embedded-Service-Processor in slot 0. • FP active—The active Embedded-Service-Processor. • R0—The route processor in slot 0. • RP active—The active route processor. • switch <number> —The switch with its number specified. • switch active—The active switch. • switch standby—The standby switch.

params.name: module
params.description: Module within the process for which the tracing level is set. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 2437 System Management set platform software trace

params.name: trace-level
params.description: Trace level. Options include: • debug—Debug level tracing. A debug-level trace message is a non-urgent message providing a large amount of detail about the module. • emergency—Emergency level tracing. An emergency-level trace message is a message indicating that the system is unusable. • error—Error level tracing. An error-level tracing message is a message indicating a system error. • info—Information level tracing. An information-level tracing message is a non-urgent message providing information about the system. • noise—Noise level tracing. The noise level is always equal to the highest tracing level possible and always generates every possible tracing message. The noise level is always equal to the highest-level tracing message possible for a module, even if future enhancements to this command introduce options that allow users to set higher tracing levels. • notice—The message is regarding a significant issue, but the switch is still working normally. • verbose—Verbose level tracing. All possible tracing messages are sent when the trace level is set to verbose. • warning—Warning messages.

usageSummary: The module options vary by process and by hardware-module.  Use the ? option when entering this command to see which module options are available with each keyword sequence.  Trace files are stored in the tracelogs directory in the harddisk: file system.

name: show platform software trace level
detail: View the trace levels for all the modules under a specific process.
syntax: show platform software trace level process slot

params.name: process
params.description: Process whose tracing level is being set. Options include: • chassis-manager—The Chassis Manager process. • cli-agent—The CLI Agent process. • cmm—The CMM process. • dbm—The Database Manager process. • emd—The Environmental Monitoring process. • fed—The Forwarding Engine Driver process. • forwarding-manager—The Forwarding Manager process. • geo—The Geo Manager process. • host-manager—The Host Manager process. • interface-manager—The Interface Manager process. • iomd—The Input/Output Module daemon (IOMd) process. • ios—The IOS process. • license-manager—The License Manager process. • logger—The Logging Manager process. • platform-mgr—The Platform Manager process. • pluggable-services—The Pluggable Services process. • replication-mgr—The Replication Manager process. • shell-manager—The Shell Manager process. • sif—The Stack Interface (SIF) Manager process. • smd—The Session Manager process. • stack-mgr—The Stack Manager process. • table-manager—The Table Manager Server. • thread-test—The Multithread Manager process. • virt-manager—The Virtualization Manager process. • wireless—The wireless controller module process. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 2440 System Management show platform software trace level

params.name: slot
params.description: Hardware slot where the process for which the trace level is set, is running. Options include: • number—Number of the SIP slot of the hardware module where the trace level is set. For instance, if you want to specify the SIP in SIP slot 2 of the switch, enter 2. • SIP-slot / SPA-bay—Number of the SIP switch slot and the number of the shared port adapter (SPA) bay of that SIP. For instance, if you want to specify the SPA in bay 2 of the SIP in switch slot 3, enter 3/2. • F0—The Embedded Service Processor in slot 0. • F1—The Embedded Service Processor in slot 1. • FP active—The active Embedded Service Processor. • R0—The route processor in slot 0. • RP active—The active route processor. • switch <number> —The switch, with its number specified. • switch active—The active switch. • switch standby—The standby switch. • number—Number of the SIP slot of the hardware module where the trace level is set. For instance, if you want to specify the SIP in SIP slot 2 of the switch, enter 2. • SIP-slot / SPA-bay—Number of the SIP switch slot and the number of the shared port adapter (SPA) bay of that SIP. For instance, if you want to specify the SPA in bay 2 of the SIP in switch slot 3, enter 3/2. • F0—The Embedded Service Processor in slot 0. • FP active—The active Embedded Service Processor. • R0—The route processor in slot 0. • RP active—The active route processor.

name: request platform software trace archive
detail: Archive all the trace logs relevant to all the processes running on a system since the last reload on the switch and to save this in the specified location.
syntax: request platform software trace archive [last number-of-days [days [target location]] | target location]

params.name: last number-of-days
params.description: Specifies the number of days for which the trace files have to be archived.

params.name: target location
params.description: Specifies the location and name of the archive file.

usageSummary: This archive file can be copied from the system, using the tftp or scp commands.

name: show platform software btrace-manager
detail: Display the most recent UTF/UTM information for a specific module.
syntax: show platform software trace filter-binary filter [ status UTF UTM ]

params.name: filter
params.description: Shows the UTF binary stream filter.

params.name: status
params.description: Shows the status of the binary trace manager filter.

params.name: UTF
params.description: Shows the UTF unified trace file.

params.name: UTM
params.description: Shows the UTM trace encoder.

name: set logging
detail: Display the time zone for logging operations.
syntax: set logging { backtrace process | marker string | ra { collect } | timezone { UTC | local } | tracelog-number process | tracelog-files-to-preserve number | tracelog-storage-quota size }

params.name: backtrace
params.description: Displays the backtrace details of a specific process.

params.name: marker
params.description: Selects the logs corresponding to the specified marker: • start last marker— The latest matching marker in the marker list. • end marker— The first matching marker after the start marker.

params.name: timezone
params.description: Sets the time zone to be displayed in trace logs. The time zone set by using the set logging command is displayed for the trace logs of show logging and monitor logging commands. If the time zone is not set for a device, then the trace logss appear in Cordinated Universal Time (UTC).

params.name: ra
params.description: Sets the RA attributes.

params.name: tracelog-number
params.description: Sets the tracelog number of a specific process.

params.name: tracelog-files-to-preserve
params.description: Set amount of files to preserve from rotation

params.name: tracelog-storage-quota
params.description: Set Tracelog files to preserve

usageSummary: In scenarios where the time zone has already been set and the trace logs need to be dispalyed in UTC, use the command set logging timezone UTC and the trace logs will appear in UTC.

name: set logging marker
detail: Add a marker trace to all the processes.
syntax: set logging marker marker-name

params.name: marker-name
params.description: Sets the marker trace in the trace logs of a process. The marker string entered is not case sensitive.

name: show logging
detail: Display the state of system logging (syslog) and the contents of the standard system logging buffer.
syntax: show logging

name: show logging process
detail: View messages logged by binary trace for a process or processes.
syntax: show logging process process-name

params.name: process-name
params.description: You can choose a certain process for which the logs need to be displayed. Example: dbm, sman, ios, btman and so on. The process name is not case sensitive.

name: show logging process-helper
detail: Display the logs for a specific process.
syntax: show logging process-helper process-name

params.name: process-name
params.description: You can choose a certain process for which the logs need to be displayed. Example: bt-logger, btrace-manager, ios, dbm, logger, and so on.

name: show logging profile
detail: Display the logs for a specific profile.
syntax: show logging profile profile-name

params.name: profile-name
params.description: • all: Displays the logs for all processes. • file: Displays the logs for a specific profile file. • hardware-diagnostics: Displays the logs for the hardware-diagnostics specific processes • install: Displays the logs for Install-specific processes. • netconf-yang: Displays the logs for netconf-yang specific processes. • restconf: Displays the logs for restconf-specific processes. • sdwan: Displays the logs for SDWAN-specific processes. • wireless: Displays the logs for Wireless-specific processes.

name: show logging profile wireless
detail: Display the logs for a specific profile.
syntax: show logging profile wireless
usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.  The following example shows how to display logs for the wireless profile.

name: show logging profile wireless end
detail: Specify log filtering end location timestamp for filtering.
syntax: show logging profile wireless end timestamp time-stamp show logging profile wireless end timestamp time-stamp

params.name: time-stamp
params.description: The timestamp to end the filtering. For example, 2023/02/10 14:41:50.849.

usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.

name: show logging profile wireless filter
detail: Specify filter for logs.
syntax: show logging profile wireless filter { interface | ipv4 | ipv6 | mac | ra | string | uuid }

params.name: interface
params.description: Selects logs with specific interface app context.

params.name: ipv4
params.description: Selects logs with specific IPv4 address app context.

params.name: ipv6
params.description: Selects logs with specific IPv6 address app context.

params.name: mac
params.description: Selects logs with specific MAC app context.

params.name: string
params.description: Selects logs with specific string app context.

params.name: uuid
params.description: Selects logs with specific Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) app context.

params.name: ra
params.description: Selects the radioactive logs.

usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.  The following example shows how to specify filter for logs: Device# show logging profile wireless filter mac ECE1.

name: show logging profile wireless fru
detail: Specify field-replaceable unit (FRU) specific commands.
syntax: show logging profile wireless fru

params.description: SM-Inter-Processor slot 0
SM-Inter-Processor slot 1

params.name: F0
params.description: Embedded-Service-Processor slot 0

params.name: FP
params.description: Embedded-Service-Processor

params.name: R0
params.description: Route-Processor slot 0

params.name: RP
params.description: Route-Processor

usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.

name: show logging profile wireless internal
detail: Select all the logs.
syntax: show logging profile wireless internal
usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.

name: show logging profile wireless level
detail: Select logs above a specific level.
syntax: show logging profile wireless level { debug | error | info | notice | verbose | warning }

params.name: debug
params.description: Selects the debug-level trace messages.

params.name: error
params.description: Selects the error-level trace messages.

params.name: info
params.description: Selects the informational-level trace messages.

params.name: notice
params.description: Selects the notice-level trace messages.

params.name: verbose
params.description: Selects the verbose-level trace messages.

params.name: warning
params.description: Selects the warning-level trace messages.

usageSummary: • Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.  • Trace level determines the types of traces outputted.

name: show logging profile wireless module
detail: Select logs for specific modules.
syntax: show logging profile wireless module module-name

params.name: module-name
params.description: List the module names separated by commas or space. For example, dbal, tdllib or "dbal tdllib".

usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.  The following example shows how to select logs for specific modules: Device# show logging profile wireless module dbal Logging display requested on 2023/03/13 08:53:08 (UTC) for Hostname: [FABRIEK], Model: [C8300-1N1S-4T2X], Version: [17.

name: show logging profile wireless reverse
detail: View logs in reverse chronological order.
syntax: show logging profile wireless reverse
usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.  The following example shows how to view logs in reverse chronological order: Device# show logging profile wireless reverse Logging display requested on 2023/03/13 08:18:40 (UTC) for Hostname: [BRU-C9K-153-05], Model: [C9300-24T], Version: [17.

name: show logging profile wireless start
detail: Specify log filtering start location.
syntax: show logging profile wireless start { last | marker | timestamp }

params.name: last
params.description: Display the logs since last event.

params.name: marker
params.description: The marker to start filtering from. It must match with previously set marker

params.name: timestamp
params.description: The timestamp for filtering. for example, "2023/02/10 14:41:50.849".

usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.

name: show logging profile wireless switch
detail: Specify the switch to look for logs.
syntax: show logging profile wireless switch { <switch-nmber> | active | standby }

params.name: Chasis-number
params.description: The chasis number.

params.name: active
params.description: Selects the active instance.

params.name: standby
params.description: Selects the standby instance.

usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.

name: show logging profile wireless to-file
detail: Decode files stored in disk and write the output to a file.
syntax: show logging profile wireless to-file output-file-name

params.name: output-file-name
params.description: Output file name. File with this name will be created in the flash/bootflash/crashinfo/harddisk memory.

usageSummary: Ensure that you enable internal keyword using the show logging profile wireless internal command to get the trace output.  Without the internal keyword, only customer curated logs are displayed.

name: show logging profile sdwan
detail: View messages logged by binary trace for Cisco-SD-WAN-specific processes and process modules.
syntax: show logging profile sdwan [ extract-pcap to-file path | [ end timestamp ts ] [ module name ] [ internal ] [ start { last { n { days | hours | minutes | seconds } clear boot } | timestamp ts } [ end { last { n { days | hours | minutes | seconds } clear boot } | timestamp ts } ] ] [ level level ] [ fru slot ] [ reverse | [ trace-on-failure | metadata ] [ to-file path ] ] ]

params.name: extract-pcap to-file path
params.description: Extracts pcap data to a file.

params.name: end timestamp ts
params.description: Shows logs up to the specified timestamp.

params.name: module name
params.description: Selects logs for specific modules.

params.name: internal
params.description: Selects all logs. start{ last { n {days | hours | minutes | Shows logs collected between the specified start and end seconds} | clear | boot} | timestamp ts}[end{ times. last { n {days | hours | minutes | seconds} | clear | boot} | timestamp ts}]

params.name: level level
params.description: Shows logs for the specified and higher levels.

params.name: fru slot
params.description: Shows logs from a specific FRU.

params.name: reverse
params.description: Shows logs in reverse chronological order.

params.name: to-file path
params.description: Decodes files stored in disk and writes output to file.

params.name: trace-on-failure
params.description: Shows the trace on failure summary.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Shows metadata for every log message.

usageSummary: Cisco SD-WAN Daemons Supported from Release • fpmd Cisco IOS XE Release 17. 4.

name: show logging profile sdwan internal
detail: View messages logged by binary trace for Cisco-SD-WAN-specific processes and process modules.
syntax: show logging profile sdwan internal

params.name: end timestamp ts
params.description: Shows logs up to the specified timestamp. start{ last { n {days | hours | minutes | Shows logs collected between the specified start and end seconds} | clear | boot} | timestamp ts}[end{ times. last { n {days | hours | minutes | seconds} | clear | boot} | timestamp ts}]

params.name: level level
params.description: Shows logs for the specified and higher levels.

params.name: fru slot
params.description: Shows logs from a specific FRU.

params.name: reverse
params.description: Shows logs in reverse chronological order.

params.name: to-file path
params.description: Decodes files stored in disk and writes output to file.

params.name: trace-on-failure
params.description: Shows the trace on failure summary.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Shows metadata for every log message.

usageSummary: Cisco SD-WAN Daemons Supported from Release • fpmd Cisco IOS XE Release 17. 4. 1a • ftm • ompd • vdaemon • cfgmgr Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.

name: show log file
detail: Display the log files in bootflash:, crashinfo:, flash:, harddisk:, or webui:.
syntax: show log file

name: monitor logging
detail: Monitor log generation in real-time for a process or a profile.
syntax: monitor logging

name: monitor logging filter
detail: Specify filter for monitorin logs.
syntax: monitor logging filter { interface | ipv4 | ipv6 | mac | ra | string | uuid }

params.name: interface
params.description: Selects logs with specific interface app context.

params.name: ipv4
params.description: Selects logs with specific IPv4 address app context.

params.name: ipv6
params.description: Selects logs with specific IPv6 address app context.

params.name: mac
params.description: Selects logs with specific MAC app context.

params.name: string
params.description: Selects logs with specific string app context.

params.name: uuid
params.description: Selects logs with specific Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) app context.

params.name: ra
params.description: Selects the radioactive logs.

name: monitor logging level
detail: Monitor logs above a specific level.
syntax: monitor logging level { debug | error | info | notice | verbose | warning }

params.name: debug
params.description: Selects the debug-level trace messages.

params.name: error
params.description: Selects the error-level trace messages.

params.name: info
params.description: Selects the informational-level trace messages.

params.name: notice
params.description: Selects the notice-level trace messages.

params.name: verbose
params.description: Selects the verbose-level trace messages.

params.name: warning
params.description: Selects the warning-level trace messages.

name: monitor logging metadata
detail: Display metadata for every log message.
syntax: monitor logging metadata

name: monitor logging process-helper
detail: Monitor log generation in real-time for a process or a profile.
syntax: monitor logging process-helper process-name

params.name: process-name
params.description: You can choose a certain process for which the logs need to be monitored. Example: bt-logger, btrace-manager, ios, dbm, logger, and so on.

name: monitor logging
detail: Monitor logs for a profile.
syntax: monitor logging profile profile-name

params.name: profile
params.description: • all: Displays the logs for all processes. • file: Displays the logs for a specific profile file (bootflash:, crashinfo:, flash:, harddisk:, or webui:). • hardware-diagnostics: Displays the logs for the hardware-diagnostics specific processes • install: Displays the logs for Install-specific processes. • netconf-yang: Displays the logs for netconf-yang specific processes. • restconf: Displays the logs for restconf-specific processes. • sdwan: Displays the logs for SDWAN-specific processes. • wireless: Displays the logs for Wireless-specific processes.
